title: Northgate Contract Playbook
status: stub — chapter content to be populated
description: The legal team's negotiation playbook: standard positions, fallback tiers and refusal points for recurring redlines on commercial contracts. Cited by section (e.g. Playbook §3.1) in contract edit logs.

chapters.title: Confidentiality & NDAs
chapters.synopsis: Standard positions for mutual NDAs: maximum term (3 years, not 5), the definition of Confidential Information (marked or reasonably-understood, standard carve-outs), and a firm prohibition on residuals clauses.

chapters.title: Commercial Terms & Pricing
chapters.synopsis: Pricing and payment positions: accepted pricing structures (including ramp schedules with pro-rated fees), standard net-30 payment terms (counter net-60 requests), late-payment interest, and refusal of most-favoured-customer/MFN commitments.

chapters.title: Liability & Remedies
chapters.synopsis: Limitation-of-liability fallback tiers (standard cap 12 months' fees; data-breach super-cap up to 2× annual fees; uncapped liability refused), excluded losses, SLA service credits as sole remedy, and floors for liability accepted from vendors.

chapters.title: Intellectual Property
chapters.synopsis: IP positions: commissioned/deliverable work must be assigned to Northgate on payment (licence-only positions reversed), and source-code escrow requests are refused with a business-continuity commitment offered instead.

chapters.title: Governing Law & Disputes
chapters.synopsis: England & Wales governing law as default, with defined fallback jurisdictions and compensating commitments (e.g. EU hosting) when a counterparty insists on another governing law; dispute-resolution and arbitration positions.

chapters.title: Term, Renewal & Exit
chapters.synopsis: Renewal mechanics and notice periods, caps on renewal price uplifts (with fallback tiers), acceptable term commitments and early-termination fees, and exit-assistance obligations at end of term.

chapters.title: Compliance & Audit
chapters.synopsis: Positions on customer/supplier compliance clauses and audit rights: scope, frequency, notice and cost allocation for audits, and boundaries on regulator-driven flow-down obligations.

chapters.title: Partner, Reseller & Referral Arrangements
chapters.synopsis: Channel positions: no exclusive appointments for partners or resellers, and standard terms for referral commissions (rates, triggers, duration).

chapters.title: Variations, Amendments & Pre-Contract Documents
chapters.synopsis: Refusal of unilateral variation rights, discipline on amendment scope (amendments limited to their stated subject), and treatment of heads of terms / MoUs (non-binding except defined provisions).

chapters.title: Escalation & Off-Playbook Approvals
chapters.synopsis: What to do when a counterparty position falls outside the fallback tiers: who can approve a deviation (Legal Director, then COO/CFO per POL-LGL-002), how deviations are recorded, and turnaround expectations for escalated points.

chapters.title: Templates & Clause Library
chapters.synopsis: Index of approved starting documents (MSA v4, mutual NDA, DPA module, order forms, referral and reseller agreements) and the approved fallback wording for each negotiable clause; rules on when counterparty paper may be used instead.

chapters.title: Playbook Governance & Change Control
chapters.synopsis: Ownership and annual review of the playbook, how positions are added or changed (evidence from deal history), version history (v3 2023 → v4 2025) and training for new legal and sales team members.
